author: boma_naps
content: Hello <@372731338031366165> <@194432762315407360> <@504282618372554752>  I have a question in regards to the versioning we have been planning on pushing - When a user visits the website and multiple versions are available, should switching versions keep them on the same method page (e.g. staying on eth_getBalance but showing how it looked in that version), or should it take them to that version's home page? Should there be a dedicated changelog or diff view showing exactly what changed between versions  such as methods added, removed, or modified  or is the version toggle alone sufficient? And should the version selector live in the top navbar as a dropdown, or somewhere else on the page?

author: .fjl
content: I think it should stay on same page, yes. Dedicated changelog view, we will use GH releases or create release notes in a MD file. Version selector should be in top bar

author: boma_naps
content: Okay I will go with this and hopefully make the presentation on monday, thank you

author: boma_naps
content: Sorry two last question should a new version be triggered automatically when a git tag like v1.0.0 is pushed, or will a maintainer kick it off manually? And for the changelog, should release notes live as a markdown file inside the repo (rendered as a page on the site) or link out to GitHub Releases?

author: .fjl
content: We will release versions manually using GH releases

author: .fjl
content: The markdown files of changelog will likely be placed into the repository
